---
title: "Raoult's Law, Dew Point, Bubble Point, and Flash Calculations Reference Page<br/>*Dew Your Bubbles Have Flash?*"
author: "DOFPro group"
format: html
html-math-method: mathjax
---

{{< include /LinkKey.qmd >}}

# Intro to *Dew Your Bubbles Have Flash?*

Raoult’s Law describes vapor–liquid equilibrium for mixtures of volatile components that behave approximately as ideal solutions. It provides the basis for calculating bubble points, dew points, and flash equilibria for many binary mixtures.

The videos on this page show how Raoult’s Law is used to calculate **bubble pressures, dew pressures, bubble temperatures, dew temperatures, and flash equilibria** for mixtures of volatile liquids.

---

## Part 1

This video introduces Raoult’s Law and demonstrates how it is used to calculate **bubble pressures and dew pressures** for binary mixtures.

<iframe width="560" height="315" src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/R4bimpGAV7o?si=IFTJpCzdfwOmqsIj" title="YouTube video player" frameborder="0" allowfullscreen></iframe>

[Visuals](74-Visuals.qmd)

---

## Part 2

This video continues with **bubble temperature and dew temperature calculations** using Raoult’s Law.

<iframe width="560" height="315" src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/9GyrfJzg-tg?si=tIbrZXZORXHYH_B2" title="YouTube video player" frameborder="0" allowfullscreen></iframe>

[Visuals](75-Visuals.qmd)

---

## Part 3

This video demonstrates **flash calculations**, where a mixture separates into vapor and liquid phases at a specified temperature and pressure.

<iframe width="560" height="315" src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/bG15qQ9gB4c?si=V6SC6uhwQXHIlHKt" title="YouTube video player" frameborder="0" allowfullscreen></iframe>

[Visuals](75.5-Visuals.qmd)

---

# Examples and Definitions

## Examples Links

- [RaoultsPxyTxyExample](https://dofprobucket1.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/material/RaoultsPxyTxyExample.ods) – A spreadsheet used for creating the *Pxy* and *Txy* diagrams in Part 3

## Definitions

Raoult's Law  
: For an ideal solution, the partial pressure of component $i$ in the vapor phase is proportional to its mole fraction in the liquid phase and the vapor pressure of the pure component.

$$
p_i = y_i P = x_i p_i^*
$$

where $x_i$ is the liquid mole fraction, $y_i$ is the vapor mole fraction, $P$ is the system pressure, and $p_i^*$ is the vapor pressure of the pure component.

Ideal Gas Mixture  
: A mixture of gases that obeys the ideal gas law and **Dalton’s Law of partial pressures**.

$$
P = \sum p_i
$$

Each component behaves as if the other gases were not present.

Ideal Solution  
: A liquid mixture in which intermolecular interactions between unlike molecules are the same as those between like molecules. Such mixtures follow Raoult’s Law over the full composition range.

Dew Pressure  
: The pressure at which a vapor of a given composition and temperature **begins to condense**.

Bubble Pressure  
: The pressure at which a liquid of a given composition and temperature **begins to vaporize**.

Dew Temperature  
: The temperature at which a vapor of a given composition and pressure **begins to condense**.

Bubble Temperature  
: The temperature at which a liquid of a given composition and pressure **begins to vaporize**.

Flash  
: A process in which a mixture with known overall composition is brought to a temperature and pressure within the two-phase region. The mixture separates into vapor and liquid phases, and the compositions and amounts of the phases are calculated.

---

# Other Links and Videos

## Other Links

## Previous and Following Videos

### Two videos back

**Show Some Flash and Dew the Dew with a Little Bubbly!**  
*Graphical BUBL P, DEW P, BUBL T, DEW T, and FLASH calculations*  
[Video](https://youtu.be/shKvpcsJIEM), [Info Page](../../balances/material/GraphicalDewBubbleFlashVideoInfoWebPage.qmd), [Visuals](../../balances/material/72-BubbleDewFlashVisuals.qmd)

### Previous video

**Oh, Henry!**  
*Gas-Liquid Equilibrium with one condensible component*  
[Video](https://youtu.be/dCOQy1C7yf0), [Info Page](../../balances/material/HenrysLawVideoInfoWebPage.qmd), [Visuals](../../balances/material/73-Visuals.qmd)

### Next video

**The Shocking Truth About Equilibrium Stages—Are You Doing It Wrong? Part 1**  
*Equilibrium Stage Equations*  
[Video](https://youtu.be/bY9fl5DJzmk), [Info Page](../../balances/material/EqullibriumStagesVideoInfoWebPage.qmd), [Visuals](../../balances/material/85-EquilibriumSeparationStages1Visuals.qmd)

### The next after next video (i.e. two videos forward)

**The Shocking Truth About Equilibrium Stages—Are You Doing It Wrong? Part 2**  
*Distillation Examples: Binary Phase Diagram, Raoult's law*  
[Video](https://youtu.be/wc6gv7lNtI0), [Info Page](../../balances/material/EqullibriumStagesVideoInfoWebPage.qmd), [Visuals](../../balances/material/86-EquilibriumSeparationStages2Visuals.qmd)
